w:r.w:t: Drop this file as CLAUDE.md in the repo root — or paste Section 2 directly into the terminal.
Partial — some routes exist, need DAI layer added
Do not ask — just code. Read existing files first, then implement.

Save this entire prompt as CLAUDE.md in the root of your langify-07 repo. Claude Code reads this file automatically at the start of every session and uses it as persistent context. You never have to paste anything — just run claude in the terminal and it already knows what to build.
git commit -m 'add claude code agent context'
Option B — Paste directly into Claude Code terminal
Copy everything from Section 2 onwards and paste it as your first message in the Claude Code terminal session. Claude Code will read it as context and begin implementing.
PASTE EVERYTHING BELOW THIS LINE INTO CLAUDE CODE
Your identity as the coding agent

You are a senior full-stack engineer implementing the Dialect-Adaptive Intelligence (DAI) feature layer for Langify — a Universal Live Speech Translation platform built on Node.js + Express + Vue.js + Supabase, using Whisper-1 and GPT-4o.

The base translation pipeline (Whisper-1 transcription -> GPT-4o translation) is partially built. Your job is to extend it with a personalization layer that makes the system learn each user's dialect, vocabulary, and emotional speech patterns over time.
Always read existing files before editing. Run: ls, cat, or Read tool on any file before touching it.
Never break existing working routes. Extend — do not replace.
Never ask permission to proceed. Read the task, read the code, implement.
If a file does not exist yet, create it. If it exists, extend it.
Commit after each major step with a descriptive message.
The repo is at: github.com/tanmay-stack07/langify-07. Assume this structure exists (verify with ls before starting):
│ │ ├── translate.js # POST /api/translate — live chunk translation <-- EXTEND THIS
│ │ └── session.js # POST /api/session/end — Supabase batch save <-- EXTEND THIS
│ │ ├── useSession.ts # Live session state <-- EXTEND THIS
│ │ └── useMediaRecorder.ts # MediaRecorder + audio capture <-- EXTEND THIS
│ ├── UtteranceCard.vue # Translation result card <-- EXTEND THIS
│ └── LiveTranslatePage.vue # Main live translate UI <-- EXTEND THIS
Before writing any code: run cat on translate.js, session.js, useSession.ts, and UtteranceCard.vue to understand their current implementation. Then implement the tasks below in order.
2.3 Task List — Implement in This Exact Order
Do each task fully before moving to the next. Commit after each.
This file must contain the exact SQL below. After creating it, run it against Supabase using the Supabase CLI or paste it into the Supabase SQL editor (note this in a comment at the top of the file):
-- Run via: supabase db push OR paste into Supabase SQL editor
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_profiles (
id UUID DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY,
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_vocabulary (
id UUID DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY,
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS session_summaries (
id UUID DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() PRIMARY KEY,
ALTER TABLE utterances ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS emotional_tone TEXT;
ALTER TABLE utterances ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS inferred_intent BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE;
ALTER TABLE utterances ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS speaker_id INTEGER DEFAULT 0;
ALTER TABLE utterances ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS alt_1 TEXT;
ALTER TABLE utterances ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS alt_2 TEXT;
Commit: git commit -m 'feat: add DAI schema migrations'
TASK 2 — Create backend/lib/dai.js — the DAI context builder
Create a new file: backend/lib/dai.js. This module is responsible for fetching the user's personal context from Supabase and building the system prompt and Whisper prompt for each translation call.
You are Langify's Dialect-Adaptive Translation Engine (DATE).
CORE MISSION: Translate speech from ANY language into the user's chosen
target language, understanding their personal accent, local vocabulary,
1. NEVER ask the user what language they are speaking. Auto-detect always.
3. If source equals target language, return original text unchanged.
4. Preserve emotional register — frustration, excitement, formality.
5. If speech is fragmented or trails off, infer the full intended meaning
from context and express it completely. Do NOT translate literally.
- Treat all vocabulary map words as valid with their mapped meanings.
- Unknown local/slang words: infer from context, flag as new_terms.
for metadata, translate entire utterance seamlessly.
- Detect: neutral | frustrated | urgent | excited | sarcastic | formal
- Reflect same register in target language via word choice.
OUTPUT — respond ONLY with this JSON, no other text:
// ── FETCH USER PROFILE + VOCABULARY FROM SUPABASE ───────────────────
// ── BUILD WHISPER PROMPT (injects known vocab for better transcription) ──
await supabase.rpc('increment_vocab_uses', { p_user_id: userId, p_term: term });
module.exports = { getUserContext, buildWhisperPrompt, buildSystemPrompt, learnNewTerms, ensureUserProfile };
Also add this Supabase RPC function to the migration file (add to 001_dai_schema.sql):
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION increment_vocab_uses(p_user_id TEXT, p_term TEXT)
UPDATE user_vocabulary SET uses = uses + 1, last_seen = NOW()
WHERE user_id = p_user_id AND term = p_term;
Commit: git commit -m 'feat: add DAI context builder lib'
Read the existing translate.js first. Then extend it to use the DAI layer. The existing Whisper + GPT-4o call chain must be preserved — you are wrapping it with context, not replacing it.
Accept userId in the request body (add it to the frontend call too)
Before the Whisper call: fetch user context via getUserContext(userId)
Pass buildWhisperPrompt(vocab) as the prompt param to Whisper
Pass buildSystemPrompt(...) as the system message to GPT-4o
Parse GPT-4o response as JSON (it now returns structured JSON, not plain text)
After translation: call learnNewTerms(userId, result.new_terms) — do not await
Return the full result object to the frontend (translatedText, detectedLanguage, confidence, emotionalTone, inferredIntent, alt_1, alt_2)
// backend/routes/translate.js — KEY CHANGES (read existing file first)
const { getUserContext, buildWhisperPrompt, buildSystemPrompt, learnNewTerms, ensureUserProfile } = require('../lib/dai');
const { audioChunk, targetLanguage, userId, sessionState } = req.body;
// 4. GPT-4o call — full DAI system prompt
// 6. Fire vocab learning async — do NOT await
Commit: git commit -m 'feat: extend translate route with DAI context'
TASK 4 — Extend backend/routes/session.js with post-session AI
After the existing Supabase batch save, fire two async post-session jobs: session summarizer and vocabulary learning. Do not break the existing save logic — add to it.
// backend/routes/session.js — ADD after existing batch save
// After res.json({ success: true, sessionId }), add:
// ── New function (add at bottom of file) ─────────────────────────────
async function runPostSessionAI(sessionId, utterances, meta, userId) {
Analyze this translation session and return ONLY JSON:
Return: { summary, main_topics, action_items, key_decisions, domain,
Commit: git commit -m 'feat: add post-session AI summarizer'
Read the existing useSession.ts. Extend it to:
Generate or retrieve a userId (use localStorage key 'langify_user_id', generate UUID if not present)
Track sessionState: { utterances: [], inferredTopic: null } — update after each utterance
Pass userId and sessionState in the body of every POST /api/translate call
Store the full result object per utterance — including emotionalTone, inferredIntent, alt_1, alt_2
Infer session topic from the first 3 utterances: after 3rd utterance, set inferredTopic to the most repeated noun/topic word (simple heuristic is fine — or call a tiny GPT-4o mini call)
import { v4 as uuidv4 } from 'uuid'; // npm install uuid
// In translate call — add to existing fetch body:
Commit: git commit -m 'feat: extend useSession with DAI context tracking'
TASK 6 — Extend UtteranceCard.vue with DAI UI elements
Read the existing UtteranceCard.vue. Add the following UI elements without removing anything existing:
Always shown. Small pill next to DetectedLanguagePill. Colors: frustrated=coral, excited=amber, formal=blue, urgent=red, sarcastic=violet, neutral=gray.
Shown only when inferredIntent === true. Small italic label: '✦ intent inferred'. Tooltip: 'Translation went beyond literal words'.
Shown when confidence < 0.72. Amber badge: 'Low confidence'. Expands to show alt_1 and alt_2 as clickable buttons.
Shown inside LowConfidenceWarning expand. Two buttons: 'Alt 1: <text>' and 'Alt 2: <text>'. Clicking one replaces translatedText for that card.
Keep the dark Jarvis palette (#13132A background, #E2E8F0 translated text) from the existing card. New pills follow the same pill style as DetectedLanguagePill.
Commit: git commit -m 'feat: add DAI UI elements to UtteranceCard'
TASK 7 — Smart Pause Detection in useMediaRecorder.ts

Read the existing useMediaRecorder.ts. Replace the fixed 5-second chunk interval with silence-boundary detection. The existing MediaRecorder setup must still work — you are changing WHEN chunks are sent, not HOW they are captured.
const SILENCE_THRESHOLD = 10; // RMS below this = silence
const MIN_CHUNK_MS = 1500; // never send shorter than 1.5s
const MAX_CHUNK_MS = 8000; // always send by 8s regardless
const rms = Math.sqrt(dataArray.reduce((s, v) => s + (v - 128) ** 2, 0) / dataArray.length);
const naturalBoundary = silenceFrames >= SILENCE_FRAMES_NEEDED && elapsed >= MIN_CHUNK_MS;
Commit: git commit -m 'feat: smart pause detection replaces fixed 5s chunks'
After all tasks are complete, run through this checklist before pushing:
Verification checklist — run each test manually
Start a live session. Speak rough Hinglish ('yaar solid tha ekdum'). Confirm translation arrives with emotionalTone field populated.
Check the UtteranceCard shows the EmotionalTonePill with correct color.
After 3 utterances, confirm sessionState.inferredTopic is set and being sent in subsequent translate calls.
Force a low-confidence scenario (whisper quietly or speak very briefly). Confirm alt_1 and alt_2 appear on the card.
End a session. Check Supabase session_summaries table has a new row.
End two sessions. Check user_vocabulary table is accumulating terms.
Start a third session. Confirm the vocabulary terms appear in the Whisper prompt (log it server-side).
Run npm test or node scripts/test.js if tests exist. All existing tests must still pass.
Final commit: git commit -m 'feat: DAI layer complete — all 8 tasks implemented'
Add these to .env if not already present:
TRANSLATION_MODEL=gpt-4o # swap to gpt-4o-mini during dev to save cost
DAI_ENABLED=true # feature flag — set false to disable DAI layer
VOCAB_MAX_ENTRIES=200 # max vocabulary entries per user profile
CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD=0.72 # below this = show alt translations
NEW — Supabase schema for DAI tables + utterance extensions
NEW — context builder, prompt templates, vocab learning
EXTEND — add DAI context injection, structured JSON parse
EXTEND — add async post-session summarizer call
EXTEND — userId, sessionState, extended result storage
EXTEND — smart pause detection replaces fixed interval
— End of Agent Prompt — Save as CLAUDE.md and run: claude
